(Redirected from [Krait (CPU)](/w/index.php?title=Krait_(CPU)&redirect=no "Krait (CPU)"))

ARM-based CPU

Krait

**[Qualcomm](/wiki/Qualcomm "Qualcomm") Krait** is an [ARM](/wiki/ARM_architecture "ARM architecture")-based [central processing unit](/wiki/Central_processing_unit "Central processing unit") included in the [Snapdragon](/wiki/Qualcomm_Snapdragon "Qualcomm Snapdragon") S4 and earlier models of Snapdragon 400/600/800 series [SoCs](/wiki/System_on_chip "System on chip"). It was introduced in 2012 as a successor to the Scorpion CPU and although it has architectural similarities, Krait is not a [Cortex-A15](/wiki/Cortex-A15 "Cortex-A15") core, but it was designed in-house.[[1]](#cite_note-1) In 2015, Krait was superseded by the 64-bit [Kryo](/wiki/Kryo "Kryo") architecture, first introduced in Snapdragon 820 SoC.

## Overview

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Krait_(processor)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

* 11-stage integer pipeline with 3-way decode and 4-way [out-of-order](/wiki/Out-of-order_execution "Out-of-order execution") [speculative issue](/wiki/Speculative_execution "Speculative execution") [superscalar](/wiki/Superscalar "Superscalar") execution
* Pipelined VFPv4[[2]](#cite_note-2)[*[under discussion](/wiki/Talk:Krait_(processor)#floating_point_version "Talk:Krait (processor)")*] and 128-bit wide [NEON](/wiki/NEON_(instruction_set) "NEON (instruction set)") ([SIMD](/wiki/SIMD "SIMD"))
* 7 execution ports
* 4 KB + 4 KB direct mapped L0 [cache](/wiki/CPU_cache "CPU cache")
* 16 KB + 16 KB 4-way set associative L1 cache
* 1 MB ([dual-core](/wiki/Dual-core "Dual-core")) or 2 MB (quad-core) 8-way set-associative L2 cache
* Dual- or quad-core configurations
* Performance (DMIPS/MHz):
  + Krait 200: 3.3 (28 nm LP)
  + Krait 300: 3.39[[3]](#cite_note-3) (28 nm LP)
  + Krait 400: 3.39 (28 nm HPm)
  + Krait 450: 3.51 (28 nm HPm)
